retrieved.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
Are you studying at another Austrian university, but would like to complete individual courses at the University of Vienna? You can have these courses recognised at your home university by applying for co-registration. Don't forget to register for courses after your application has been approved.

Please note:

- You cannot complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna as a co-registered student.
- The co-registration has to be applied for each semester.
- If you are enrolled in a Doctoral/PhD programme and want to co-register, please contact the [Center for Doctoral Studies](https://doktorat.univie.ac.at/en/doctoralphd-programmes/co-registration/).

If you are studying at the University of Vienna and would like to take courses at another university, please contact the [StudyServiceCentre (SSC) or the StudyServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme.

You need the following documents as PDF files:

- [the pre-approval form (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Formulare/Pre-approval_of_the_co-registration.pdf)
- the student record sheet of the current semester (the semester, in which you want to co-register)
- your student ID card, valid passport or personal ID

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application period: from 13 July to 31 Oktober 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

retrieved.title: Registration for courses with continuous assessment
retrieved.text:
Seminars, exercises e.g. are courses with continous assessment (German: prüfungsimmanente Lehrveranstaltung, pi LV). Courses with continuous assessment require a registration. In courses with continuous assessment attendance and other requirements are compulsory. The registration deadlines run in the weeks before the semester starts, i.e. in September for the winter semester and in February for the summer semester. If you are registered for a course with continuous assessment and are absent from the first unit without having a good reason, you will be deregistered.

With the registration to a course, you are automatically registered for the respective Moodle course.

- Some courses have a limited number of participants.
- Places are allocated in different ways, which are determined by each [directorate of studies](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273314) (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL)
- Either through the [point allocation system](#c600152), the [preference system](#c600156) or the [recommended path of study](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/recommended-path-of-study/).
- Students on the [waiting list](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273323) will be accepted if course places become available. In case you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course, as there may be free places.

retrieved.text: The registration period for the course I would like to complete ends earlier than the application period for co-registration. What deadline do I have to meet? You can only register for a course once the co-registration has been approved. The processing period can take up to two weeks. You should therefore submit your application for co-registration in good time. - When do I get access to the Moodle courses? First, wait for the approval of the co-enrolment. Afterwards, register for the desired course. Only then will you have access to the Moodle course. Please note that the course must be activated by a lecturer or tutor so that it is also accessible to students. It is up to the course instructor to decide when this happens. - Why do I need a signed pre-approval? The signed pre-approval allows your home university to check whether the selected courses can be recognised for your degree programme. Your home university will issue it if it is not possible to take certain examinations there (e.g. because the course is not offered there). Please contact your home university to find out which office is responsible for confirming your pre-approval. - Where can I find the student record sheet of the University of Vienna?? You can download your student record sheet as soon as the co-registration has been approved. You can find the student record sheet

With the point allocation system, you receive a certain number of points (= points account) every semester which you can use for the registration. You will have a points account for the registration for courses and another one for the registration for exams.

If you register for courses offered by different directorates of studies, you will have several points accounts. The points can be used for courses with a limited number of participants.

The more points you allocate to a course, the more likely it is that you are accepted.

The allocation of points can still be changed within the [registration deadline](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741417). You cannot save points for the following semester, which means that you will lose points that you have not used.

You can find further information in the [ABC of terminology.](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741407)

Please note that you have to complete an [introductory and orientation period (STEOP)](/en/semester-planning/introductory-and-orientation-period-steop/) at the beginning of every bachelor’s programme before you are allowed to register for any other courses. The following page provides instructions on how to register for courses and exams. In order to register for courses and exams, you need an active [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) as well as a valid [admission](/en/admission-procedure/) to a degree programme at the University of Vienna.

[Registration for courses](#c254735)
[Registration for exams](#c254738)

- For information about how to plan your schedule, please go to the website [Planning your semester](/en/semester-planning/).

- The [ABC of terminology](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1) provides explanations on the university terminology.

reranked.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
Are you studying at another Austrian university, but would like to complete individual courses at the University of Vienna? You can have these courses recognised at your home university by applying for co-registration. Don't forget to register for courses after your application has been approved.

Please note:

- You cannot complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna as a co-registered student.
- The co-registration has to be applied for each semester.
- If you are enrolled in a Doctoral/PhD programme and want to co-register, please contact the [Center for Doctoral Studies](https://doktorat.univie.ac.at/en/doctoralphd-programmes/co-registration/).

If you are studying at the University of Vienna and would like to take courses at another university, please contact the [StudyServiceCentre (SSC) or the StudyServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme.

You need the following documents as PDF files:

- [the pre-approval form (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Formulare/Pre-approval_of_the_co-registration.pdf)
- the student record sheet of the current semester (the semester, in which you want to co-register)
- your student ID card, valid passport or personal ID

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application period: from 13 July to 31 Oktober 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

reranked.title: Registration for courses with continuous assessment
reranked.text:
Seminars, exercises e.g. are courses with continous assessment (German: prüfungsimmanente Lehrveranstaltung, pi LV). Courses with continuous assessment require a registration. In courses with continuous assessment attendance and other requirements are compulsory. The registration deadlines run in the weeks before the semester starts, i.e. in September for the winter semester and in February for the summer semester. If you are registered for a course with continuous assessment and are absent from the first unit without having a good reason, you will be deregistered.

With the registration to a course, you are automatically registered for the respective Moodle course.

- Some courses have a limited number of participants.
- Places are allocated in different ways, which are determined by each [directorate of studies](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273314) (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL)
- Either through the [point allocation system](#c600152), the [preference system](#c600156) or the [recommended path of study](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/recommended-path-of-study/).
- Students on the [waiting list](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273323) will be accepted if course places become available. In case you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course, as there may be free places.

reranked.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
reranked.text: The registration period for the course I would like to complete ends earlier than the application period for co-registration. What deadline do I have to meet? You can only register for a course once the co-registration has been approved. The processing period can take up to two weeks. You should therefore submit your application for co-registration in good time. - When do I get access to the Moodle courses? First, wait for the approval of the co-enrolment. Afterwards, register for the desired course. Only then will you have access to the Moodle course. Please note that the course must be activated by a lecturer or tutor so that it is also accessible to students. It is up to the course instructor to decide when this happens. - Why do I need a signed pre-approval? The signed pre-approval allows your home university to check whether the selected courses can be recognised for your degree programme. Your home university will issue it if it is not possible to take certain examinations there (e.g. because the course is not offered there). Please contact your home university to find out which office is responsible for confirming your pre-approval. - Where can I find the student record sheet of the University of Vienna?? You can download your student record sheet as soon as the co-registration has been approved. You can find the student record sheet

With the point allocation system, you receive a certain number of points (= points account) every semester which you can use for the registration. You will have a points account for the registration for courses and another one for the registration for exams.

If you register for courses offered by different directorates of studies, you will have several points accounts. The points can be used for courses with a limited number of participants.

The more points you allocate to a course, the more likely it is that you are accepted.

The allocation of points can still be changed within the [registration deadline](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741417). You cannot save points for the following semester, which means that you will lose points that you have not used.

You can find further information in the [ABC of terminology.](/en/abc-of-terminology/?no_cache=1#c741407)

selected.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
selected.text:
Are you studying at another Austrian university, but would like to complete individual courses at the University of Vienna? You can have these courses recognised at your home university by applying for co-registration. Don't forget to register for courses after your application has been approved.

Please note:

- You cannot complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna as a co-registered student.
- The co-registration has to be applied for each semester.
- If you are enrolled in a Doctoral/PhD programme and want to co-register, please contact the [Center for Doctoral Studies](https://doktorat.univie.ac.at/en/doctoralphd-programmes/co-registration/).

If you are studying at the University of Vienna and would like to take courses at another university, please contact the [StudyServiceCentre (SSC) or the StudyServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme.

You need the following documents as PDF files:

- [the pre-approval form (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Formulare/Pre-approval_of_the_co-registration.pdf)
- the student record sheet of the current semester (the semester, in which you want to co-register)
- your student ID card, valid passport or personal ID

Winter semester 2026/27:

- Application period: from 13 July to 31 Oktober 2026
- Semester start: 1 October

Summer semester 2027:

- Application period: from 11 January to 31 March 2027
- Semester start: 1 March

[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

selected.title: Registration for courses with continuous assessment
selected.text:
Seminars, exercises e.g. are courses with continous assessment (German: prüfungsimmanente Lehrveranstaltung, pi LV). Courses with continuous assessment require a registration. In courses with continuous assessment attendance and other requirements are compulsory. The registration deadlines run in the weeks before the semester starts, i.e. in September for the winter semester and in February for the summer semester. If you are registered for a course with continuous assessment and are absent from the first unit without having a good reason, you will be deregistered.

With the registration to a course, you are automatically registered for the respective Moodle course.

- Some courses have a limited number of participants.
- Places are allocated in different ways, which are determined by each [directorate of studies](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273314) (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL)
- Either through the [point allocation system](#c600152), the [preference system](#c600156) or the [recommended path of study](/en/registration-for-coursesexaminations/recommended-path-of-study/).
- Students on the [waiting list](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273323) will be accepted if course places become available. In case you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course, as there may be free places.

You can only register for a course once the co-registration has been approved. The processing period can take up to two weeks. You should therefore submit your application for co-registration in good time. - When do I get access to the Moodle courses? First, wait for the approval of the co-enrolment. Afterwards, register for the desired course. Only then will you have access to the Moodle course. Please note that the course must be activated by a lecturer or tutor so that it is also accessible to students. It is up to the course instructor to decide when this happens. - Why do I need a signed pre-approval? The signed pre-approval allows your home university to check whether the selected courses can be recognised for your degree programme. Your home university will issue it if it is not possible to take certain examinations there (e.g. because the course is not offered there). Please contact your home university to find out which office is responsible for confirming your pre-approval. - Where can I find the student record sheet of the University of Vienna??
